creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
> You point out using the README that the upstream hasn't moved,
They moved their source repository, but README file in its current form (that's what displayed on GitHub) contains less information than their still functional homepage on Google Code.  This probably won't last, but for the moment https://code.google.com/archive/p/osd-lyrics/ gives better idea and overview of the project.

> but their homepage (google code) states otherwise and you did acknowledge that.
It states that repository has been moved to GitHub.

> What do you consider "the project homepage" if not what has been google code?
Project page is what I read to get idea of what the project is.  Repository is where they're keeping the source code.

I'm not saying that WWW will point to Google Code indefinitely, just that while it's functional and more complete than README file I'd rather not do one-line commits.
